question.text: Post listing of an InvIT, prior approval of unit holders is necessary if the total value of all related party transactions pertaining to acquisition or sale of assets exceeds what percentage of the value of InvIT assets?

answer.text: 15% of the value of InvIT assets
5% of the value of InvIT assets
25% of the value of InvIT assets
10% of the value of InvIT assets

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under InvIT Regulations, prior approval of unit holders is necessary if the total value of all related party transactions pertaining to acquisition or sale of assets or investments into securities exceeds 5% of the value of InvIT assets in a financial year. Similarly, borrowing from related parties exceeding 5% of total consolidated borrowings also needs unit holder approval. **The Trap:** 10% is the corresponding threshold for REITs, not InvITs. InvITs have a more conservative 5% threshold. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** InvIT related party threshold = 5% vs REIT = 10%. The lower threshold for InvITs reflects the more concentrated nature of infrastructure assets.
